$comment: Codex CLI integration for JEWEL1k agent-key. Codex has no PreToolUse-style blocking hook; what it does have is top-level `notify` in ~/.codex/config.toml, which runs a program with one JSON argument per notification. Put it before any [projects.'...'] table, or it may become a project-table value instead of the global notify setting. Point it at `agent-key hook codex-notify` (ships with the CLI): `agent-turn-complete` turns the LED green (done); unknown notification types are ignored. For richer states, wrap Codex in a shell alias that runs the `wrapper_*` commands below around the session. See docs/HOOKS.md.

wrapper_commands.$comment: Optional: run these around a Codex session (shell alias/wrapper) until Codex grows lifecycle hooks.

manual_approval.$comment: Codex approvals stay in the terminal; to additionally gate a risky command on the physical key, run this from a script and treat non-zero exit as deny (0=approve, 2=deny, 3=timeout).
command: Codex wants to run a command
